observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to coordinate their efforts in securing the Bank Statements
on time that will result in the timely submission of the Bank Reconciliation
Statements within the prescribed period of ten (10) days after the end of the
month as required under COA Circular No. 96-011.

2.6 We recommend that Management direct the Municipal engineer to strictly
comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2013-004 as regards notification
or publicity of the projects of the Municipality.

We recommend that Management submit the purchase orders and other contracts within the five-day reglementary period from the execution and issuance thereof as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

1.6 We recommended that Management submit the purchase orders and
other contracts within the five-day reglementary period from the
execution and issuance thereof as required under COA Circular No.
2009-001.

We recommended that Management comply with the submission of Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and complete documentary requirements related thereto of government motor transportations in pursuance to Section 361 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual.

We recommended that Management comply with the submission to the Auditor’s Office copies of POs and AIRs relative to the procurement of supplies and materials for the repairs and maintenance of local schools within five (5) working days from the execution thereof and within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of goods, respectively, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

1.5 We recommended that Management comply with the submission of
Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and complete documentary
requirements related thereto of government motor transportations in
pursuance to Section 361 of Government Accounting and Auditing
Manual.

2.5 We recommended that Management comply with the submission to the
Auditor’s Office copies of POs and AIRs relative to the procurement of
supplies and materials for the repairs and maintenance of local schools
within five (5) working days from the execution thereof and within twenty-
four (24) hours from acceptance of goods, respectively, pursuant to COA
Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 in order that the Confirmation Letter, duly signed and approved by the Regional Director, be the one submitted to the Auditor to acquire the status of a bonded accountable officer.

We recommended that Management comply with sub-paragraph c (24) Section 12 of Republic Act No. 10121, or the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010 regarding submission of report on the utilization of the DRRM fund.

We recommended that Management submit Purchase Orders and other contracts within the five (5) -day period after execution and issuance thereof as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

1.7 We recommended that Management strictly comply with the pertinent
provisions of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 in order that the
Confirmation Letter, duly signed and approved by the Regional Director,
be the one submitted to the Auditor to acquire the status of a bonded
accountable officer.

2.3 We recommended that Management comply with sub-paragraph c (24)
Section 12 of Republic Act No. 10121 or the Philippine Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Act of 2010 regarding submission of report
on the utilization of the DRRM fund.

3.6 We recommended that Management submit Purchase Orders and other
contracts within the five (5) -day period after execution and issuance
thereof as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February
12, 2009.

We recommended that Management adopt payment of salaries and other benefits thru automated bank scheme to strengthen its internal control on cash and to promote operational efficiency. Coordination with government authorized depository bank to implement the payment of payrolls thru ATM should be immediately undertaken.

We recommended that Management formulate a concrete Waste Management Plan in accordance with the “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000” or R.A. 9003 by setting guidelines and targets for solid waste avoidance and volume reduction through waste reduction at source and waste minimization measures, and embark on effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s ecological solid waste management program to sustain economic development that is in harmony with the environment.

We recommended that Management adopt payment of salaries and other benefits thru automated bank scheme to strengthen internal control on cash and to promote operational efficiency.  Coordination with government authorized depository bank to implement the payment of payrolls thru ATM should be immediately undertaken.

We recommended that Management formulate a concrete Waste Management Plan in accordance with the “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000” or R.A. 9003 by setting guidelines and targets for solid waste avoidance and volume reduction through waste reduction at source and waste minimization measures, and embark on effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s ecological solid waste management program to sustain economic development that is in harmony with the environment.
The management should continue the practice of complying with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the LDRRMF pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

The management should create an Inventory Committee to be responsible in the conduct of physical inventory-taking of the assets owned by the municipality as well as the preparation of the required reports pertaining thereto and to submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommend that the concerned officers should strictly comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the requirement to submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year containing the required information.

Submission of contracts and Purchase Orders within the five-day period after execution and issuance thereof, respectively, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 should be strictly adhered to by the management.
The management should continue the practice of complying with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the LDRRMF pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

The management should create an Inventory Committee to be responsible in the conduct of physical inventory-taking of the assets owned by the municipality as well as the preparation of the required reports pertaining thereto and to submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommend that the concerned officers should strictly comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the requirement to submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year containing the required information.

Submission of contracts and Purchase Orders within the five-day period after execution and issuance thereof, respectively, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 should be strictly adhered to by the management.
